name: Time Freeze
description: A freeze suspends selected actors, projectiles, hazards, or simulation channels while exempt systems continue running.
design_notes: A freeze mechanic needs a strict affected_channel_mask and exemption rules before content is built around it. Designers should decide whether the player can move, whether collisions with frozen bodies are solid, how queued impulses resume, and which clocks continue for UI, audio, networking, and scripted objectives.
design_purpose: Create tactical windows where the player can reposition, inspect, or set up delayed interactions.
Support puzzles that depend on storing motion, hazards, or enemy state at a chosen moment.
Give encounters a dramatic pause without fully entering a menu or cutscene state.
player_fantasy: The player feels like they can hold the world still while planning the next decisive action.

parameters.name: freeze_scope_id
parameters.description: Scope definition that chooses which entities and simulation channels are suspended.

parameters.name: affected_channel_mask
parameters.description: Simulation channels paused by the freeze while exempt channels continue advancing.

parameters.name: freeze_window_ms
parameters.description: Milliseconds the suspension stays active before release rules restore affected channels.

parameters.name: exemption_rule_id
parameters.description: Data rule that defines actors or systems allowed to keep updating during the freeze.

parameters.name: release_blend_ms
parameters.description: Blend window used to resume animation, particles, and stored rigidbody velocities smoothly.

edge_cases: A frozen rigidbody is moved by the player, then released with stale velocity or overlapping collision shapes.
A freeze starts while an AI, animation montage, or ability transaction is halfway through a state transition.
Objective timers, cooldowns, and rhythm charts disagree about whether the freeze should pause them.
Server-authoritative multiplayer must choose whether all clients see the freeze or only the triggering player does.
common_bugs: Frozen actors still receive damage ticks, status effects, or scripted callbacks from unpaused systems.
Release restores velocity but not angular velocity, sleeping state, animation phase, or particle emission state.
Hit detection runs against the visually frozen pose while the authority simulation has already resumed.
Nested freeze effects overwrite each other's saved state and release too early.
balancing_notes: A full global freeze is usually stronger than a slow-motion effect because it removes enemy reaction entirely.
Bosses and objectives may need resistance or shortened windows, but exemptions should be visible and consistent.
Let players understand what will resume with stored momentum so puzzle outcomes feel earned rather than random.
accessibility_notes: Avoid intense flashing, sudden audio dropouts, or heavy color inversion as the only freeze indication.
If the freeze creates planning space, provide enough UI clarity for players using slower input methods.

implementation_notes.unity: Do not rely only on Time.timeScale = 0 for selective freezes. Store per-entity freeze_state_id, Rigidbody velocity, angular velocity, sleep state, Animator speed, and particle playback state, then restore through a release transaction that validates overlaps.
implementation_notes.godot: Use process_mode, physics processing toggles, and explicit state caches for frozen nodes. RigidBody and CharacterBody objects need velocity and contact state restoration, while UI and exempt actors should remain on an unscaled clock.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use component-level tick disabling, Custom Time Dilation, movement mode suspension, and physics body state snapshots rather than pausing the entire world. In replicated play, the server should approve freeze_scope_id and replicate release timestamps to clients.
implementation_notes.web: Tag simulation channels with frozen or exempt state in the update loop. Matter.js, Arcade Physics, or custom solvers should skip integration for frozen bodies but keep cached transforms, velocities, and collision masks available for release validation.

relationships.reason: Time freeze is an extreme timing-control variant that fully pauses selected gameplay clocks.
Shared sessions need authority rules for which clients, actors, and replicated clocks are frozen.
Brief freeze frames can emphasize successful counters while preserving combat readability.

example_games.title: The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild
example_games.note: Uses stasis-like freezing on objects for puzzle and combat interactions.

example_games.title: Superhot
example_games.note: Uses extreme time manipulation as the central action premise.
